title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #1, Section 1 Prologue. Credit given in the MoMA Master Checklist as 'Lick Observatory' (institution, not an individual photographer); no nationality listed. Print size 120 x 89 cm. Title deprivation per CNA education portal; no title in checklist.

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #2, Section 1 Prologue. Wynn Bullock, American. Print size 50 x 63 cm. Widely identified in secondary sources as Bullock's 'Let There Be Light' (1954); the MoMA Master Checklist itself does not record a title or date. The MoMA permanent-collection record for Bullock's 'Let There Be Light' is works/55534; identification with plate 2 is reported rather than verified plate-to-object.

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #3, Section 1 Prologue. Credit reads: 'from "Art in the Ice Age"' — a book source rather than a single named photographer. Print size 20 x 17 cm. No photographer name or date in checklist.

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #4, Section 1 Prologue. Nat Farbman, LIFE magazine, American. 20 x 17 cm. (Belgian Congo is the 1955 geopolitical designation used by the checklist; today Democratic Republic of the Congo.)

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #6, Section 1 Prologue. The MoMA checklist skips numbers 5, 7, and 8 — whether this reflects withdrawn plates, reserved numbers, or internal edits is not stated in the document. We have compacted the gap in our 1-50 row ids: photo-0005 corresponds to checklist #6. Wynn Bullock, American, 140 x 177 cm. Widely identified in secondary sources as Bullock's 'Child in Forest' (1951); MoMA permanent-collection record works/55598. Plate-to-object identification is reported rather than primary-verified.

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #9, Section 1 Prologue. Harry Callahan, American, 24 x 23 cm. Numbers 5, 7, 8 are absent from the Prologue section of the checklist (see photo-0005 notes).

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #10, Section 1 Prologue. Pat English, LIFE, American nationality per checklist. Entry is a two-panel piece: 104 x 26 cm and 33 x 114 cm.

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #11A, Section 1 Prologue. Eugene Harris, POPULAR PHOTOGRAPHY, American, 14 x 18 cm. The catalog uses this Peruvian flute-player image as a recurring coda at three points (#11A in Prologue, #11B above #74 in Children A, #11C below #193 in Woman's Work). We record only the #11A instance here.

Miller's three-plate sequence (42, 43, 44) in Section 5 is widely reported in secondary literature as documenting the birth of his own son in 1946; we do not record that provenance here (separate provenance issue per catalog-builder brief).

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #49, Section 7 Births. R. Diament, Moscow Journalists Club, Russian per checklist, 12 x 8 cm. USSR is the 1955 geopolitical designation used by the checklist.

title: Untitled
notes: Checklist #50, Section 7 Births. Elliott Erwitt, Magnum, American per checklist, 14 x 21 cm. This is the final entry in the first 50 checklist numbers; Section 8 'Mothers and Babies' begins at #51. See photo-0005 notes on the numbering gap — the MoMA Master Checklist uses numbers 1-4, 6, 9-50 (skipping 5, 7, 8) across plates 1-50 of its sequence, so we seed 47 CSV rows from the first 50 checklist numbers.
